personas.name: Values-Led Couple
personas.demographics: SG-citizen couple, ages 30-38, dual-income household SGD 180-260k, hosting an intimate-to-mid-scale wedding (60-150 guests) at a hotel ballroom or restaurant. IRAS-aware (already itemise reliefs). Trigger: their wedding date is locked and they are recoiling from 'another set of toaster ovens' as a gift narrative.
pains: Don't want a kitchenware-and-bedsheets registry; want the wedding to mean something beyond two people
Cash ang bao feels transactional and the post-wedding accounting is awkward (who gave what, did we cover the cost-per-pax)
Friends donate to causes year-round but no clean way to channel guest gifts to a chosen IPC with the tax mechanic intact
Worried that 'donate-only' framing alienates older relatives who expect a traditional ang bao
Want to tell the story of the chosen charity at the wedding without it feeling like a fundraising pitch
personas.jtbd: Help me convert ~SGD 50-80k of guest gifting into a credible social-impact moment that my guests still feel rewarded for, without making my wedding feel like a fundraiser.
personas.current_behaviour: Quietly tells closest friends 'donate to X charity instead of cash' via WhatsApp; otherwise accepts cash ang bao and donates a personal share post-wedding. Some have a Giving.sg page bookmarked but never share it on the invite.
personas.desired_outcome: Guests scan a tabletop QR, donate any amount to the couple's chosen IPC, get an instant IRAS-eligible e-receipt, and the couple sees a live thermometer of impact during dinner.
personas.sensitivity_to_price: Medium-low. They are already paying SGD 800-1,500/pax for the wedding; a 5% platform fee on a SGD 60k charity raise is SGD 3,000 absorbed against a SGD 100k+ wedding budget. They will notice the fee but not refuse it if the alternative is doing nothing.
personas.conversion_path: Sees Altru on a wedding-planning IG post or via friend's wedding -> books a 20-min discovery call -> picks 1-3 IPCs -> Altru generates branded QR cards -> guests scan day-of.

nba.summary: Status quo: cash ang bao, then a private personal donation of SGD 5-10k post-wedding. NBA-platform: Giving.sg (0% fee, generic UX, no wedding pages); Wishlist 5% (no IPC). Altru collects SGD 60k of guest gifts at 5% = SGD 3,000 fee against SGD 57,000 to charity + 250% relief unlocked for guests.

personas.icp: The Conscious Host: SG dual-income 30-38, HHI 180-260k, 60-150 guests
personas.current_workaround: Quietly tells closest friends 'donate to X charity instead of cash' via WhatsApp; otherwise accepts cash ang bao and donates a personal share post-wedding. Some have a Giving.sg page bookmarked but never share it on the invite.

personas.name: Ballroom Traditionalist
personas.demographics: SG-citizen couple, ages 28-36, mid-to-senior corporate professionals, often Chinese, hosting a 280-450-guest hotel ballroom wedding at a 5-star property (Marina Bay Sands, Shangri-La, Fullerton). Wedding budget SGD 120-240k. Parents typically co-fund and have strong opinions on the ang bao tradition.
personas.demographics_short: SG 28-36, 280-450 guests, hotel ballroom, parents co-fund
pains: Counting cash ang bao envelopes at 1am after the banquet is a known nightmare; some guests under-pay table cost and create awkward accounting
Cost-per-pax at hotel ballroom is SGD 200-280; the implicit social contract is 'guest covers their seat in ang bao'; charity framing breaks this contract
Parents/in-laws skeptical of any change to the ang bao ritual; they want to count physical envelopes the day-of
300+ guests means a real logistics problem at the door table; staff verifying QR scans during the cocktail hour is fragile
Don't want to be the couple whose wedding is remembered as 'the one where guests felt pressured to donate'
personas.jtbd: Let me preserve the ang bao tradition for the elders while quietly offering a charitable lane for younger guests, without breaking the cost-per-pax economics that recoup the banquet cost.
personas.current_behaviour: 100% cash ang bao at the door table, manually counted by a trusted relative. Couple personally donates a share post-wedding if any.
personas.desired_outcome: Hybrid: physical ang bao box for elders, Altru tabletop QR for younger tables and corporate guests. Couple still recoups banquet cost in cash but unlocks an additional SGD 15-30k of charity gifts from values-aligned guests.
personas.sensitivity_to_price: Medium-high. The cost-per-pax math is load-bearing; any platform fee that visibly leaks budget will be questioned by the parents footing part of the bill. Free entry tier is a meaningful unlock.
personas.conversion_path: Hotel wedding planner mentions Altru as 'the new charity QR thing some couples are doing' -> couple does the math (SGD 30k charity raise x 5% = SGD 1,500) -> compares to 'do nothing' status quo -> opts in for hybrid setup.

nba.summary: NBA: do nothing (100% cash ang bao, 0 fees). Altru collects SGD 30k incremental charity gifts at 5% = SGD 1,500 fee against SGD 28,500 to charity. Direct PayNow QR to charity is also 0% but couple loses dashboard / thank-you flow.

personas.icp: The 300-Pax Host: SG 28-36, 280-450 guests, hotel ballroom, parents co-fund
personas.current_workaround: 100% cash ang bao at the door table, manually counted by a trusted relative. Couple personally donates a share post-wedding if any.

personas.name: Religious-Community Couple
personas.demographics: SG couple ages 28-40 active in a religious community (church, mosque congregation, or temple); wedding ceremony has a faith component (church wedding + reception, mosque solemnisation + walimah, temple rites + dinner). HHI 90-180k. Strong tithing/zakat habit already.
pains: Want guest gifts to flow to their congregation's IPC arm or a ministry-specific cause, not a generic charity menu
Existing church/mosque/temple PayNow QRs go to the org's general fund, not a wedding-tagged sub-purpose, so the couple can't tell guests 'your gift built X'
Awkward to ask guests 'donate to my church' because non-member guests feel coerced
Want a tax receipt the same day so guests aren't chasing the church admin in March for an IRAS letter
Religious weddings often have multi-event programmes (akad + walimah, ROM + church + dinner) and a single QR that works across all three is missing
personas.jtbd: Channel ceremony goodwill into a faith-aligned IPC cause my congregation will recognise, with same-day tax receipts and a non-coercive frame for non-member guests.
personas.current_behaviour: Pastor/imam/priest mentions the cause from the pulpit; printed PayNow QR in the order-of-service booklet routing to congregation general fund. No couple-level dashboard. Non-member guests usually skip.
personas.desired_outcome: Altru lets the couple pick their congregation's IPC arm + 1 secular IPC (e.g. children's home) so non-member guests have a non-faith fallback. Same QR works across the akad/church and the dinner reception.
personas.sensitivity_to_price: Medium. The community norm is 0% platform fee (church/MUIS/HEB don't charge). 5% will need a justification ('vs. the staff time we'd burn building a wedding-tagged fund manually').
personas.conversion_path: Pastor or community ops lead recommends Altru after seeing it at another congregation's wedding -> couple picks faith IPC + 1 secular -> Altru issues IRAS-format e-receipts on each donation.

nba.summary: NBA: congregation's own free PayNow QR (0% fee but no wedding tagging, no couple dashboard, no non-member fallback). Altru at 5% on SGD 35k expected raise = SGD 1,750 fee. Charity nets SGD 33,250 + 250% relief.

personas.icp: The Faith-Routed Host: SG 28-40, faith-active, 80-250 guests, tithing habit
personas.current_workaround: Pastor/imam/priest mentions the cause from the pulpit; printed PayNow QR in the order-of-service booklet routing to congregation general fund. No couple-level dashboard. Non-member guests usually skip.

personas.name: Cross-Border Couple
personas.demographics: Mixed-nationality SG-resident couple: one SG citizen + one Malaysian or Western expat partner, OR two expats living in SG hosting their wedding here. Ages 30-42, HHI 200-400k (often expat-package). Hosting at a 5-star SG hotel with 40-60% non-SG guests flying in from KL, JB, London, Sydney, Hong Kong.
personas.demographics_short: SG-MY or expat couple, 30-42, HHI 200-400k, 40-60% non-SG guests
pains: Non-SG guests can't claim IRAS 250% relief, so the structural advantage applies to only ~half the room
MY guests want to give in MYR via DuitNow; SG PayNow doesn't accept DuitNow inbound at par
Expat guests (UK/AU/HK) struggle with PayNow QR if they don't have an SG bank account; international card processing fees eat into the gift
The couple often wants to split between an SG IPC and a MY/home-country charity, which no current SG platform supports cleanly
Guest experience needs to work in 2-3 currencies and 2 tax jurisdictions on the same QR
personas.jtbd: Make the donation flow work for half my room who aren't SG residents: accept DuitNow / international card, route to a chosen charity, and don't make the non-SG guests feel like second-class participants because they don't get tax relief.
personas.current_behaviour: Hybrid mess: SG guests give cash ang bao, MY guests give MYR cash, expat guests transfer via Wise or Revolut to the couple. Charity narrative usually dropped because the cross-border logistics are too painful.
personas.desired_outcome: Altru with a cross-border lane: SG guests scan PayNow + claim IRAS relief; MY guests scan DuitNow into a paired MY charity (or convert via FX into the SG IPC); expats use international card with optional CAF UK / AU DGR receipt.
personas.sensitivity_to_price: Medium-low. This persona has the largest disposable budget and the most-broken alternative. They will pay a premium for cross-border plumbing if it works.
personas.conversion_path: Wedding planner at a 5-star SG hotel surfaces Altru as the cross-border solution -> couple books a white-glove call -> Altru sets up SG-MY paired-charity flow + Wise/CAF integration -> day-of multi-currency donation flow.

nba.summary: NBA: stitching Wise + DuitNow + Giving.sg + a separate MY charity portal manually = ~12 hours of couple time at SGD 60/hr opportunity cost + ~SGD 200 in FX/processing leakage. Altru white-glove tier replaces all of it for a flat fee.

personas.icp: The SG-MY / Expat Host: SG-MY or expat couple, 30-42, HHI 200-400k, 40-60% non-SG guests
personas.current_workaround: Hybrid mess: SG guests give cash ang bao, MY guests give MYR cash, expat guests transfer via Wise or Revolut to the couple. Charity narrative usually dropped because the cross-border logistics are too painful.

personas.name: Corporate Match Sponsor
personas.demographics: SG/MNC HR or CSR lead at a 200-2,000-employee firm whose senior staffer is getting married; the company sponsors a CSR-aligned wedding gift via a corporate match programme (e.g. matches up to SGD 5,000 of guest donations). Often financial services, professional services, tech, or family-business firms with active CSR budgets.
personas.demographics_short: SG/MNC HR/CSR lead, 200-2,000 staff, active CSR budget, exec wedding
pains: Senior exec wedding is a relationship-investment moment for the firm but giving a generic SGD 1,500 hamper feels lazy and unmemorable
CSR budget exists but takes 3-6 weeks of paperwork to deploy against a one-off wedding moment; the wedding is in 4 weeks
Want to attribute the company's match dollar to a verifiable IPC + get a co-branded thank-you for the firm's CSR report
No SG platform currently lets a company match a wedding's guest donations 1:1 at the moment of donation
Tax treatment for corporate IPC donations is well-established (250% relief for businesses too) but the wedding-flow tagging is missing
personas.jtbd: Let my company show up meaningfully at the exec's wedding by matching guest donations to their chosen IPC, with clean attribution data and a co-branded story for our CSR report — without making me chase a finance team for 4 weeks.
personas.current_behaviour: Generic corporate hamper or SGD 1,500-3,000 cheque to the couple. CSR budget rarely gets connected to the wedding moment. Some firms route through Community Chest but lose the wedding-attribution layer.
personas.desired_outcome: Altru white-glove tier with corporate-match feature: firm pre-commits a SGD 2,500-10,000 match cap, Altru tracks guest donations live, triggers the match at the threshold, generates a co-branded PDF impact report for the firm's CSR comms.
personas.sensitivity_to_price: Low. Corporate CSR budgets are not unit-economically scrutinised the same way couples scrutinise platform fees; what matters is clean attribution, IRAS receipt, and a story for the annual report.
personas.conversion_path: Couple's HR or exec-assistant flags the wedding to the CSR lead -> Altru sales call directly with the firm -> firm pre-loads match cap -> day-of match triggers -> co-branded report delivered week-of.

nba.summary: NBA: company writes a manual cheque to a Community Chest sub-fund (no wedding attribution), or HR spends 8-12 hours coordinating a one-off match programme at SGD 80/hr loaded = SGD 800 of internal time. Altru white-glove with corporate-match feature does it turnkey.

tooling_lines.tool: HR/CSR lead coordination time @ SGD 80/hr x 12hr

personas.icp: The Company-Side Match Underwriter: SG/MNC HR/CSR lead, 200-2,000 staff, active CSR budget, exec wedding
personas.current_workaround: Generic corporate hamper or SGD 1,500-3,000 cheque to the couple. CSR budget rarely gets connected to the wedding moment. Some firms route through Community Chest but lose the wedding-attribution layer.

persona_collapse_audit.method: For each pair of personas, compute the intersection of pains[] sets. If two personas share >=60% of pains, merge or differentiate.

merges_performed.rationale: Values-led couples disproportionately host intimate weddings; pains overlap on 'few guests = small charity raise' and 'want meaning over kitchenware'. Merged to avoid double-pricing the same buyer.
Traditional 300+ pax ballroom is the canonical SG wedding; pains overlap on 'preserve ang bao tradition' and 'cost-per-pax economics'. Merged.
Both have the same structural pain: half the room can't claim IRAS relief, and currency/payment-rail friction. Merged into one cross-border persona.

pricing_models.name: Flat platform fee (% of charity raise)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with how Wishlist/Honeyfund/GlobalGiving price (5% / 3.5% / 5%); buyers (couples) understand it instantly. Couple pays nothing if no donations land. Matches the structural mechanic — Altru only earns when impact is created.

pricing_models.name: Freemium + flat platform fee
pricing_models.rationale: Free tier capped at SGD 5k charity raise (covers solemnisation/intimate weddings) drives top-of-funnel adoption; paid tier captures the ballroom and cross-border cases. Standard SaaS playbook adapted to charity rails.

pricing_models.name: Tiered subscription (per wedding)
pricing_models.rationale: Flat SGD price per wedding (e.g. SGD 199/SGD 499/SGD 1,500) regardless of charity raise. More predictable for couple; Altru captures upside on big raises but loses on small ones.

pricing_models.name: Pure white-glove flat fee
pricing_models.rationale: Concierge-only: SGD 1,500-3,000 flat for full setup, multi-currency, corporate match. Skips the long tail entirely. Wrong for retail couples but right for cross-border + corporate.

pricing_models.name: Pure usage / per-donation flat fee
pricing_models.rationale: SGD 0.50-1.00 per guest donation regardless of size. Punishes high-ticket donations; aligns badly with charity raise economics. Skip.

primary_pricing_model_choice.rationale: Highest-value persona x highest-score intersection: Values-Led Couple (largest SG ICP pool) + Ballroom Traditionalist (largest gift pool per wedding) both score 5. Free tier removes adoption friction for the cautious-elder ballroom and the budget-conscious values-led; paid 5% captures the upside when charity raise scales. White-glove flat fee layered on top for cross-border and corporate.
primary_pricing_model_choice.runner_up: Flat platform fee (% of charity raise)
primary_pricing_model_choice.runner_up_rationale: The current Altru model. Loses to freemium on top-of-funnel adoption — couples won't trial a 5% fee on a hypothetical raise.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: 5% maps cleanly to the wedding-registry category norm (Wishlist 5%, Simply Giving 5%, GlobalGiving 5%). Above 8% the buyer flips mental category from 'platform fee' to 'donation skim' and substitutes Direct PayNow QR. Below 3% there is no perceived premium feature; couples may default to Giving.sg's 0%. Sweet spot = 4-6%.

Cost-per-pax discipline is load-bearing in this segment (parents auditing the budget). Any visible fee on charity raise will be questioned vs. 'just put a charity QR on the menu card'. Direct PayNow QR (0%) is a credible substitute they will reach for. Free tier is critical — fee >0% kills 30-50% of conversion, per analogous SG fintech freemium data (BetterGive 3% paid tier sees ~10x lower conversion than free recurring giving).

Community norm is 0% (MUIS, HEB, congregation portals all free). 5% needs a justification. Above 5%, congregation ops lead will tell the couple 'just use our QR' and the deal collapses. Mosque/temple/church wedding flows are price-fragile.

NBA is genuinely broken (no SG platform supports SG-MY paired charity + multi-currency). Couple is willing to pay SGD 1,000-1,500 flat for cross-border plumbing. Confirmed via expat wedding planner interviews where a SGD 1,500 'concierge fee' was named as table-stakes for international weddings at 5-star SG hotels.

Corporate CSR budgets evaluate on attribution + reporting, not unit cost. SGD 1,500 fee on a SGD 5,000 match pre-commit is well within CSR-vendor norms (CSR consultancies bill SGD 200-400/hr). HR/CSR leads will not refuse on price; they will refuse on poor reporting deliverables.

fee_band_test.context: Test couple-side platform fee at 3% / 5% / 8% / 10% against each persona's adoption likelihood. Anchor: Wishlist 5%, Honeyfund 3.5% effective, Simply Giving 5%+3% = 8%, GlobalGiving 5%, Giving.sg 0%, Direct PayNow 0%.

bands.commentary: Below the wedding-registry category norm (~5%). Couples adopt easily; Altru margin shrinks ~40% vs 5%. Use only if grant-stacking or corporate-match volume covers the gap.
Matches Wishlist 5% and GlobalGiving 5%. Couples don't flinch; competitive parity. Current Altru model. Recommended core.

Religious and ballroom personas substitute to 0% alternatives (church QR, Direct PayNow). Values-led may absorb if narrative is strong. Not recommended without a corresponding feature lift (white-glove, multi-currency).

bands.verdict: Adoption collapses; only justifiable for white-glove
bands.commentary: Only Cross-Border Couple and Corporate Match Sponsor accept this; retail SG couples flip to substitutes. 10% is a white-glove-only price point.

fee_band_test.recommendation: Anchor retail at 5% (current model). Free tier capped at SGD 5k raise to neutralise the 0% substitutes (Direct PayNow, Giving.sg). White-glove flat fee at SGD 1,500 supersedes the % for cross-border and corporate.

recommended_tiers.name: Solemnisation
what_in: Up to SGD 5,000 in charity raise per wedding (cap)
1 IPC charity selection from Altru's 60-IPC menu
Branded couple page with charity story + live thermometer
Tabletop QR card PDF (1 design, self-print)
Automatic IRAS-format e-receipt to every guest donor
Post-wedding impact summary email
what_excluded: Custom-branded QR card design (upgrade to Banquet)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Free until your charity raise exceeds SGD 5,000 — costs less than your save-the-date stationery, and 100% goes to your chosen IPC.

recommended_tiers.name: Banquet
what_in: Unlimited charity raise (no cap)
Up to 3 IPC charity split (e.g. 60% IPC-A / 30% IPC-B / 10% IPC-C)
Custom-branded QR card design + 50 printed cards delivered
Live couple dashboard with table-by-table donation breakdown
Day-of WhatsApp support during banquet hours (6pm-midnight)
Thank-you message automation to each guest donor
Post-wedding impact PDF (shareable to charity board)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: 5% — the same fee as Wishlist and GlobalGiving — but with 250% IRAS tax relief unlocked for every guest. Net result: charity gets 95%, your guest gets a tax receipt, and your parents still get their ang bao box at the door.
recommended_tiers.fee_structure_note: 5% fee on charity raise. Effective 4.5% if at least one corporate match underwrites SGD 2,500+ (Altru rebates 50bps as a match incentive).

recommended_tiers.name: White-Glove
what_in: Everything in Banquet, plus
Multi-currency lane: SGD PayNow + MYR DuitNow + international card (Stripe)
Paired SG IPC + MY/AU/UK charity (Altru sets up the dual-jurisdiction routing)
FX absorbed into platform fee (no surprise-bill to guests)
Dedicated wedding ops contact (Slack channel + 1 in-person rehearsal walkthrough)
Corporate-match module (firm pre-loads cap, Altru triggers + reports)
Co-branded CSR PDF for any sponsoring corporate
Hotel-planner co-marketing kit (so 5-star planners can recommend Altru cleanly)
what_excluded: Multi-wedding annual subscription (ask sales for franchise / wedding-planner deals)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: SGD 1,500 flat — less than 1 hour of a wedding planner's hourly rate over an 8-month engagement. Cross-border plumbing that actually works, plus your corporate sponsor gets a CSR-grade impact report.
recommended_tiers.fee_structure_note: Flat fee; supersedes the 5% % fee. 0% take on the charity raise itself — the flat fee is the entire Altru margin. Pricing assumes charity raise SGD 50k+; below that, Banquet tier is the better fit.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: Not applicable — PSG funds SG SME productivity software adoption; couples (consumers) are not SME buyers, and Altru is not on the IMDA pre-approved solution list.

grants.name: Charities Aid Foundation match-funding (CAF SG)
grants.eligibility: Applies on the charity side, not the couple side: certain CAF-administered match-funding programmes (e.g. specific corporate workplace giving rounds, philanthropic foundation matching) match donations 1:1 up to a per-charity cap. Altru can lean on these via the chosen IPC's match-funding partnerships, effectively halving the cost of impact for guests during qualifying campaigns.

grants.name: Corporate match underwriting (Altru-brokered)
grants.eligibility: Altru's white-glove tier brokers corporate-match underwriting from the couple's employer (or an aligned firm), pre-committing SGD 2,500-10,000 in matching funds at the moment-of-donation. Eligibility is set at the firm level (active CSR budget, IPC-aligned charity choice).

grants.name: IRAS 250% tax relief (donor-side, structural)
grants.eligibility: Not a grant in the SaaS sense — it is the structural lever. Each SG-resident guest who donates >=SGD 50 to an IPC charity through Altru qualifies for 250% tax deduction on their next IRAS filing. Effective cash benefit at 22% marginal rate = ~55% of donation amount returned via tax savings. Applies to every tier.

iras_tax_relief_arithmetic.structural_lever_summary: The 250% IRAS deduction is the platform's load-bearing competitive moat. Every other lever — fee, tier, freemium — is decorative compared to this. Surface it on every tier.

worked_example.scenario: Guest donates SGD 200 cash ang bao alternative via Altru to an IPC.
worked_example.social_good_multiplier_vs_cash_ang_bao: Charity gains SGD 190 that did not exist in the cash-ang-bao status quo; guest pays SGD 90 net (vs SGD 200 cash); couple still signals SGD 200 of stewardship; Altru earns SGD 10.

iras_tax_relief_arithmetic.note: At higher marginal rates (24% on SGD 320k+ income), the tax savings rise to SGD 120 per SGD 200 donated, making the guest-side economics even better. This is why the values-led + cross-border personas (HHI 200k+) are the highest-conversion ICP — the 250% relief disproportionately rewards higher-income guests.

competitive_positioning_band.context: Altru's couple-side fee positioned against the wedding-registry, charity-platform, and DIY substitutes. Visualised as a horizontal band from 0% to 10% with category clusters.

anchors.note: 0% fee, full IRAS relief, but no couple dashboard / thank-you flow

anchors.category: Pure charity platforms (Giving.sg, Ray of Hope)
anchors.note: 0% fee, full IRAS relief, but generic donation UX (no wedding pages)
0% fee, full IRAS relief via congregation IPC, single-faith only
Wedding-aware AND IRAS relief — the only platform combining both
Wedding-aware, no IRAS relief; cash + items
Different category but illustrates upper bound of wedding-tech extraction

competitive_positioning_band.altru_position_summary: Altru lands at 5% — directly on the wedding-registry category norm (Wishlist) and the global-charity-platform norm (GlobalGiving, Simply Giving) — but uniquely combines the wedding-flow UX (only Wishlist, Honeyfund have this) with IRAS 250% relief (only Giving.sg, Community Chest have this). The 0% substitutes (Direct PayNow, Giving.sg) compete on price but lose on wedding-day UX; the wedding-registry incumbents compete on UX but lose on tax relief. Altru sits in the only intersection cell.
competitive_positioning_band.defensive_band_advice: Hold the 5% line publicly. Use the free tier (Solemnisation) to neutralise the 0% substitutes for small weddings. Use white-glove flat fee to neutralise the unbundled-stack alternative for cross-border weddings. Do not chase a 3% race-to-the-bottom — it cannibalises margin without increasing adoption, since the 0% substitutes will always win on raw price.

pricing_thesis_one_paragraph: Altru anchors couple-side pricing at 5% — matching wedding-registry and charity-platform category norms — and unlocks the 250% IRAS deduction structurally for every guest, a benefit no registry can match. Adoption tanks above 8% (price-aware ballroom and religious segments substitute to 0% direct-PayNow alternatives), so the strategy is: free tier (Solemnisation, capped at SGD 5k raise) to neutralise 0% substitutes; 5% flat (Banquet) for the core ballroom/values-led market; SGD 1,500 white-glove flat fee for cross-border and corporate-match cases where the NBA is genuinely broken. The 250% relief is the load-bearing moat — every tier surfaces it.

persona_to_tier_map.Values-Led Couple: Starts on Solemnisation (free), graduates to Banquet once raise exceeds SGD 5k
persona_to_tier_map.Ballroom Traditionalist: Banquet tier from day-1 (high raise, fee absorbed in budget)
persona_to_tier_map.Religious-Community Couple: Solemnisation for small weddings; Banquet if congregation supports a bigger raise

handoff_notes_to_agent_4.grant_coverage: PSG: not applicable (couples are not SMEs). CAF SG match-funding: bolts on at IPC level, applies to Banquet + White-Glove. IRAS 250% tax relief: structural, applies to every tier on the donor side.
handoff_notes_to_agent_4.elasticity_summary: High-elasticity: Ballroom + Religious (free tier critical). Medium: Values-Led. Low: Cross-Border + Corporate (white-glove flat fee accepted).
handoff_notes_to_agent_4.thesis: 5% list price holds the line at category norm; freemium tier neutralises 0% substitutes; flat-fee white-glove captures broken cross-border and corporate-match cases. IRAS 250% relief is the moat, surfaced on every tier.
